optimist.name: Marlene Holmqvist
optimist.column:
The Twins won yesterday, and that’s the kind of thing that makes me feel like maybe, just maybe, the prayers I’ve been saying since last fall are starting to take root. You know, the ones that went something like, “Lord, please let them find their way back to the playoffs, and this time, let them stay.” It’s not that I’ve been ignoring the standings or the fact that they’re still in a tight race, but when the scoreboard lights up with a 7-4 victory, it’s hard not to think that the universe is nudging us toward something bigger.  

Buxton’s knees feel good this week and that’s all the forecast I need. Minnesota nice is about to turn into Minnesota nasty, mark it down. I’ve been watching him lately, the way he’s been moving through the lineup, his swing smoother than a Sunday morning hymn. There’s a rhythm to his game now, like he’s not just playing baseball but conducting a symphony of hope. And let’s be honest
